items.title:
✈️💳 Why HSBC Premier is one of the most simple airmiles-based credit cards to have?

Here are a few under-appreciated benefits of the HSBC Premier Credit Card 👇

1️⃣ Every spend is included (except fuel)

No need to apply your mind over MCCs.

There is a ₹1 lakh monthly spend limit to earn points on certain categories such as utilities, tax, rent & real estate, insurance, jewellery, e-wallets, etc.

But still, ₹1 lakh per month is a pretty good limit. 👍

2️⃣ No milestone-linked benefits, not even for fee waiver

That’s the biggest relief. 😌

Milestones are arguably the most prominent reason due to which credit card users end up overspending.

A lot of mental maths, planning and strategy goes into achieving milestone-based rewards before the deadline.

In that way, HSBC Premier gives you complete peace of mind.

3️⃣ Every penny counts for rewards

While almost all airmiles-based credit cards credit reward points on spends in multiples of ₹100, ₹150 or ₹200, HSBC cards consider every single rupee of your spend for reward points.

While you earn 3 points per ₹100 spend with HSBC Premier Credit Card, if there is any residual spend or a transaction is less than ₹100, they add this spend amount to your next transaction for rewards calculation.

Wow, itna kaun karta hai! 😃

4️⃣ Reward points are credited within 2–3 days of the transaction and can be redeemed immediately ⚡

No need to wait for statement generation.

5️⃣ You can see your entire reward points statement in the app itself 📱

Better transparency and easier tracking of points earned on every transaction.

6️⃣ Points are transferred to all airline and hotel partners almost instantly ✈️🏨

No need to wait for days to see the points in your airline or hotel account.

For an airmiles-based card, instant transfer is a very big advantage as award seats can disappear anytime while you wait for miles to reflect in your account.

7️⃣ No limit on points transfer to any airline or hotel partner

No need to track Group A, Group B, transfer caps 😃 

8️⃣ One of the lowest forex mark-ups among popular premium credit cards 🌍

Most popular premium credit cards like Infinia, EPM and Magnus charge 2.36% forex markup. Atlas charges 4.13%.

HSBC Premier charges just 1.17% forex mark-up and gives unrestricted rewards on forex spends.

Irrespective of net returns in terms of points earned, HSBC Premier reduces real cash outflow due to its low forex markup.

9️⃣ HSBC Premier reward points never expire ♾️. While most premium cards provide a 3-year window to use points and expiry is generally not an issue, no expiry of points further adds to the simplicity of this credit card.

✨ Sometimes, simplicity itself is the biggest benefit.

And HSBC Premier does a great job of keeping things simple, transparent and rewarding.

#creditcards

items.title:
🤩 Two Primary HS-BC Premier Credit Cards Basis Joint Savings Account: Part-3

🤔 How am I planning to maintain ₹50 Lakhs TRV going forward?

1️⃣ Option-1: Maintain ₹50 Lakhs in Savings Account or FDs

Simple option, but the opportunity cost is huge. 💸

❌ Not going for that.

2️⃣ Option-2: Buy ₹50 Lakhs Worth of Mutual Funds Through HS-BC

👉 In this option, each of the two cardholders may have to maintain a separate ₹50 Lakhs TRV, as mutual funds are usually held individually (not sure about joint holding of MFs).

👉 You can buy mutual funds from HS-BC. Mutual funds from most AMCs are available and it does not have to be an HS-BC mutual fund.

👉 However, these will be Regular Mutual Funds, not Direct Mutual Funds. Expense ratios will be 0.5%–1% higher compared to direct MFs.

Can Existing Mutual Funds Be Transferred?

✅ Yes.

👉 Existing Regular Mutual Funds can be transferred from your current distributor to HS-BC.

❌ Direct Mutual Funds cannot be transferred.

❌ I am not opting for MF-based TRV as all my mutual funds are Direct Mutual Funds.

❌ Also, I manage my MF portfolio on my own and prefer investing only through Direct Plans.

3️⃣ Option-3: Transfer Existing Stocks, ETFs & SGBs to HS-BC

👉 You can transfer existing stocks, ETFs and even non-demat SGBs to HS-BC.

⚠️ But remember, HS-BC currently provides only a Demat Account.

❌ They do not offer a Trading Account as of now.

This means that after transferring your holdings to HS-BC, you won’t be able to instantly sell them like you do on Zerodha, Groww, etc.

👉 You can still sell them or transfer them back to another demat account through an offline request to your RM. The process may take around 5-7 days every time you wish to sell a stock or ETF.

👉 Till about a year ago, HS-BC had a tie-up with ICICI Securities for trading services. Customers could buy and sell securities held in the HS-BC Demat Account through ICICI Trading.

❌ That arrangement is no longer there.

👉 HS-BC is expected to launch its own trading account in the future, but there is no certainty on timelines.

✳️ How Much Time Do I Get to Restore TRV?

👉 HS-BC reviews TRV on a quarterly basis (calendar quarter).

If there is a shortfall at the end of a quarter:

💰 Penalty = 0.2% of the shortfall amount, subject to a maximum of ₹2,000 (₹2,360 including GST). Not a huge amount. Also, a good RM can get first penalty waived-off (as told by my RM)

👉 If TRV is still not maintained after the second quarter (6 months), the same penalty will be charged again.

⚠️ Continued non-maintenance beyond 6 months may result in downgrade of the Premier relationship.

What If I Maintain TRV for Only One of us?

👉 Since both of us are part of the same Family Banking Group, both credit cards will continue even if I maintain TRV for only one. 

However, at the time of renewal:

✅ Only one card’s annual fee will be waived.

❌ The second card will attract an annual fee of ₹23,600 including GST.

✳️ Which Option Have I Chosen?

👉 I will be transferring approximately ₹55 Lakhs worth of ETFs, some long-term stocks and some non-demat SGBs to the HS-BC Demat Account.

💡 Why?

Because this is the only zero additional cost option for me.

👉 If HS-BC launches a trading account before renewal, I may transfer another ₹50 Lakhs worth of ETFs and stocks and continue with both primary cards.

👉 Or, I may simply close one of the cards before the renewal and will take an add-on card.

😊 One primary card + one add-on card should serve the purpose perfectly.

All the lifestyle benefits such as airport transfers, meet & greet, lounge access, golf etc are available at Family Banking group level. So, entire family shares the benefits. So, no issues on that front as well ✅✅✅.

items.title:
🤩 Received Joining Bonus Points on BOTH the Primary HS-BC Pre-mier Credit Cards! 🎉

👉 Total Points Received: 2 × 20,000 = 40,000 Points 🎁
👉 Accor Value of the Points = ₹88,000 (₹2.20 per point) 🏨✨
👉 Value may multiply even further in the upcoming Accor Bonus Offer 🚀
👉 Total Joining Fee Paid: ₹14,160 × 2 = ₹28,320 💳
👉 TRV Opportunity Cost: ₹50 Lakhs deposited for 6 days at 1.31% post-tax interest: Net loss in Interest (8.55% - 1.31%) = ₹5,951 📉

📊 In summary: Got ₹88,000 worth of Accor points (which may multiply further) at a total cost of ₹34,271 😍

✳️ How did I get TWO Primary HS-BC Pre-mier Credit Cards on a single TRV of ₹50 Lakhs 🤔 ?

👉 2 Primary Cards for both joint account holders are issued only when the required deposit-based TRV is maintained in a joint savings account.

👉 When ₹50 Lakhs is deposited in a joint savings account, the amount is considered to be owned by both account holders. Therefore, HS-BC treats it as ₹50 Lakhs TRV for each joint account holder.

👉 This allowed me to get 2 Primary Pre-mier Credit Cards with a single ₹50 Lakhs deposit. 🎯

👉 HS-BC also considers Demat holdings (Shares, ETFs, SGBs, etc.) and Mutual Funds towards the ₹50 Lakhs TRV requirement 📈 (Only non-demat SGBs can be moved to HS-BC Demat).

👉 However, Demat holdings and Mutual Funds are generally held in an individual’s name and therefore cannot be counted as TRV for two people, even if they have a joint Pre-mier account.

👉 So, if TRV is maintained through Demat/MFs, each individual needs to maintain ₹50 Lakhs TRV separately. Otherwise, only the person maintaining the ₹50 Lakhs TRV will be eligible for the Pre-mier Credit Card, not both. ⚠️

👉 The salary credit route for a Pre-mier account will also make only the salary earner eligible for the Pre-mier Credit Card 💼.

👉 I maintained ₹50 Lakhs in the joint savings account for just 6 days and withdrew the amount after card approval. ⏳

👉Now, how am I planning to maintain ₹50 Lakhs TRV going forward? 🤔
👉 How much time do I have to restore the TRV for both of us?
👉 What happens if I can maintain the TRV for only one of us and not both?
👉 Will both cards continue, get downgraded, or be impacted differently?

That deserves a separate detailed post! 😊

📢 Stay tuned and follow SpendWisely if you haven’t already. 🚀

items.title:
🚨 HDFC SmartBuy (and Infinia) Devalued? 🤔💳

👉 HDFC has capped grocery voucher purchases (Amazon Fresh, BigBasket, Blinkit, even Zomato) at just ₹2,000 per month through SmartBuy. 🛒🚫

😲 Earlier, users could purchase up to ₹25,000 worth of Amazon Fresh and other grocery vouchers, making it a popular way to maximize Reward Points on everyday spends. 🎯💰

👉 For Infinia users like me, this comes as a major shocker. 💳⚡😳

🙄 The funniest part is that there is a ₹2,500 denomination Amazon Fresh Gift Card 🎁 on SmartBuy which I cannot purchase!

Why? 🤔

Because, as per HDFC, I have already exhausted my monthly buying limit of ₹2,000 on Amazon Fresh. 🚫🛒

Really? 😅 Without actually buying anything, I have exhausted my limit because your limits are lower than the Gift Card denomination available on your own portal. 🤦‍♂️🤯

👏 This is the experience HDFC wants to give to its premium card holders? 👑💳

📉 For last sometime, we have seen a series of devaluations across various HDFC Credit Card benefits. The biggest concern is not just the devaluation itself, but the increasing unpredictability around benefits and reward structures. ⚠️📊

🤷‍♂️ For a credit card where HDFC is now expecting either:

🔹 ₹18 Lakhs annual spend 💸
🔹 ₹50 Lakhs Total Relationship Value (TRV) 🏦💰

…such frequent changes are certainly disappointing. 😡🤬

🎁 Gift Cards at 5X Reward Points are the only differentiating factor in the HDFC Infinia Credit Card 💳✨today.

The rest of the benefits and reward rates are below average if you compare them with other super-premium credit cards, even HDFC’s own Diners Black Metal is better if you ignore the giftcards benefits on Infinia. 📉

With a 4.13% surcharge 💸 and minuscule, impractical limits like ₹2,000 on some of the most useful Gift Cards 🛒, HDFC is slowly killing this differentiator as well. ⚠️

🚪 Clearly, HDFC is making the dilemma of moving to sunrise super-premium cards like HSBC Premier much easier with these devaluations. 💎

🙋‍♂️ I am certainly not going to spend ₹18 Lakhs to retain Infinia.

My backup card stack is already ready:

✅ HSBC Premier 💎
✅ ICICI Emeralde Private Metal (EPM) 🏆
✅ Axis Atlas ✈️🌍

💬 What’s your credit card strategy going forward? 🤔

items.title:
🎉 Finally received 2 Primary HS-BC Pre-mier Credit Cards (for self & spouse) basis a joint Pre-mier Account of me and my wife 😎💳💳

👉 Activated both the cards and completed the first transaction on each 🛒✅. Now waiting for 20,000 joining bonus points on both cards 🎁🎁. HS-BC generally credits the joining bonus points within 3 days ⚡.

💎 My journey to HS-BC Premier twins 😎

📞 👉 Contacted HS-BC RM over phone.

🏠 👉 HS-BC RM visited my place for account opening formalities and biometrics. I handed over a cheque of ₹5 Lakhs as the initial deposit.

⚡ 👉 Account got activated in just 2 days and the ₹5 Lakhs cheque was deposited into our newly opened joint savings account.

💰 👉 Interest rate on HS-BC Pre-mier Savings Account is just 2% p.a. At my 34.32% tax bracket (including surcharge), the net post-tax return works out to only around 1.31%. Since the opportunity cost of funds for me is much higher 📈, I immediately withdrew the amount.

📝 👉 Thereafter, my RM initiated the process for Pre-mier Credit Cards for both me and my wife 💳💳.

🔍 👉 Once the application reached the TRV verification stage, he advised me to maintain ₹50 Lakhs in the savings account.

🏦 👉 I transferred ₹50 Lakhs into the Pre-mier Savings Account on 26th May.

🚀 👉 The card application got approved and the card numbers became visible in the HS-BC App on 1st June 📱✨. The RM helped in fast-tracking the application on my request, as keeping ₹50 Lakhs parked at an effective 1.31% return carries a significant opportunity cost 💸.

⏳ 👉 In total, I maintained ₹50 Lakhs in the account for just 6 days. The entire process was handled efficiently and with precision by my RM 👏👏👏. Couldn’t have expected anything better from him 🙌.

📦 👉 Received both credit cards yesterday 🎉💳💳.

🛒 👉 Immediately activated them and completed the first transaction via Amazon Now grocery ✅ to fulfil the joining benefit condition (a transaction of any amount within 30 days of card issuance is required to receive the joining bonus 🎁).

💳 Credit Limit

📌 HS-BC Premier Credit Card’s credit limit is directly linked to the TRV maintained and is generally around 20% of the TRV.

💰 👉 Accordingly, I was informed that a total credit limit of ₹10 Lakhs was available against ₹50 Lakhs TRV.

⚖️ 👉 Instead of splitting it equally between the two cards, I requested my RM to allocate ₹8 Lakhs on one card and ₹2 Lakhs on the other.

👍 👉 HS-BC accommodated the request and provided the limit split exactly as requested.

🤔 What about TRV ??

1️⃣ How will I maintain the ₹50 Lakhs TRV going forward?  

2️⃣ How much time will I get to arrive on the required TRV ??

3️⃣ Do I need to main overall Rs.50 Lacs TRV or Rs.50 Lacs for each of the card holder ? 

🧐 That deserves a separate detailed post. Stay tuned… I will share my strategy in the next day or two 😉🔥

items.title:
🚨 Why You Should NOT Fall for the HSBC x Apple 6X Offer – A Contrarian View

Before you rush to grab the deal (or worse, give in to FOMO 😅), here are a few things worth considering.

👉 The Offer: HSBC is offering 6X Reward Points on Apple purchases made through the HSBC-Unicorn online portal till 31st July

🛒 Portal: https://exclusiverewards.unicornstore.in

⚠️ The offer is NOT applicable on regular Unicorn stores or the generic Unicorn website.

💳 Eligible Cards:
✅ HSBC Prive
✅ HSBC Premier
✅ HSBC Visa Platinum
✅ HSBC RuPay Platinum

❌ HSBC Travel One Card is not eligible.

Now, let’s talk about the deal breakers👇

👉 This offer makes meaningful sense only for Premier and Prive cardholders. For Platinum cards, the value-back isn’t very exciting.

😄 And let’s not even discuss the Prive Card because 99.9999% of credit card users aren’t eligible for it.

👉 HSBC Premier Cardholders earn 18 Reward Points per ₹100 spent, which can translate into:

🏨40% value-back when redeemed on Accor

✈️ Around 18% value-back is relatively easy even through other redemption options, including HSBC’s 1 Point = ₹1 on flights/hotels.

✅✅✅ 40% value-back is massive. No doubt about that.

👉 Bonus points are capped at 12k Points per calendar month

📌 This means you can purchase ₹1.33 lakh worth of Apple products in June and another ₹1.33 lakh in July and still earn full bonus points

⚠️ HSBC will consider the settlement date for reward capping.

So:

🔸 Purchases on 29th-30th June may get counted in July

🔸 Purchases on 30th-31st July may miss the offer if settlement happens after the offer period

👉 Better to complete purchases well before last date

⏳ Bonus Reward Points will be credited within 45 days.

⚠️ If you are counting on the recently announced HSBC-to-Accor transfer bonus promotion, there is a possibility that the promotion may come and go before bonus points are credited. The last Accor transfer bonus offer lasted only for 5 days.

📢 Rumours of HSBC discontinuing Accor transfers caused many people to empty their HSBC points balances. Just a day later, HSBC announced an Accor transfer bonus offer.

🤔 HSBC may not want customers to enjoy both the Apple offer and a future Accor transfer bonus on the same spend.

Now let’s talk about Apple pricing on HSBC Unicorn vs other platforms 💰

👉 Pricing on HSBC Unicorn is reasonable and not highly inflated. Still, it’s possible to get 5-10% real savings elsewhere through more Instant discount and other card offers.

If I had to compare cash savings and Accor points value, I would discount the value of Accor points by at least 30%.

That means, if I am saving ₹70 in real cash vs ₹100 worth of Accor points, I would go for the real cash savings.

If you are not an Accor fan like me, discount the rupee value of Accor points even more.

💵 Remember, cash is king. Accor points can only be used for Accor hotels, while cash can be used for Accor as well as all other kinds of spending.

Basis the above principle, compare your savings.

And don’t forget the massive upcoming festive sale season 🎉

🗓️ Flipkart Big Billion Days and Amazon Great Indian Festival sales are expected on 11th October.

By then:

📱 iPhone 18 would likely be launched.

📉 Current-generation iPhones could already see a 10-15% price correction.

🎯 And then comes the massive festive sale discount

For context:

🔥 In Big Billion Days 2025, 

iPhone 16 Price dropped from ₹72k to ₹48k. ➡️ 33% savings

📱 iPhone 16 Pro Price dropped from ~₹1.1 lakh to ₹70k ➡️ 39% savings

🔥 In Big Billion Days 2021, I got an iPhone 12 at ₹44k vs a pre-sale price of ₹68k. ➡️ 35% real savings

The point?

🤷High possibility that Apple products see 35-40% discounts during festive sales from current price

If you can wait 4 months, getting 40% value in Accor points today may not necessarily be the best deal.

🚫 Assess your need and urgency, and then decide. Don’t fall for FOMO.

items.title: Airport Meet & Greet Service in India
items.snippet: Airport Meet & Greet is a new(er) concept in India. You are greeted upon arrival at the airport at the curbside/cab drop-off point and assisted all the way till boarding aircraft. Basics of Airport Meet and Greet We get a hassle-free experience for navigating through the airport with Meet and Greet.
